food_subgroup: Noodle dishes with seafood and meat toppings

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Indomie Deluxe. FOODON_00002473 (noodle dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Noodles, cooked) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for Indomie Deluxe with mixed seafood and meat toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific restaurant noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One jumbo plate of Indomie Deluxe as served at The Noodle Place, approximately 450 g, comprising a large portion of dry stir-fried Indomie noodles topped with scallops, chicken cheese balls, squid rings, and mixed vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: Fried noodles with mixed ingredients — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and HPB Singapore nutrient reference values for similar noodle dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match found for Indomie Deluxe with scallops, chicken cheese ball, squid rings, and vegetables. Nutrient values estimated by component analysis: Indomie goreng base (HPB Singapore reference for fried instant noodles, ~170 kcal/100g), seafood toppings (USDA scallops and squid data), chicken cheese ball (estimated from USDA chicken and cheese data), and mixed vegetables. Values represent a weighted composite per 100 g of the assembled dish at approximately 450 g total serving weight. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Instant noodles, cooked — GI reference from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Indomie Deluxe dry noodle dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Indomie Deluxe as served at The Noodle Place. GI of 53 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked instant noodles (GI approximately 52–54, low category). The dry preparation method and mixed protein/fat toppings (scallops, chicken cheese ball, squid) are expected to maintain or slightly lower the effective GI relative to plain boiled noodles. GL per serving calculated as: (53 × 83.25 g available carbohydrate per 450 g serving) / 100 = 44.1. gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; approximately 2340 mg per full 450 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from instant noodle seasoning and sauces, which can significantly exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 53) with high total glycemic load per serving (~44) due to large noodle portion
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the GI of this dish is in the low range, the large serving size results in a high glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume a reduced portion and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium, moderate saturated fat, and cholesterol from seafood and cheese ball toppings
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in sodium and contains saturated fat and dietary cholesterol from the chicken cheese ball and seafood toppings; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption, and request reduced seasoning where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Indomie Deluxe is a premium dry noodle dish served at The Noodle Place in Singapore, featuring a jumbo portion of Indomie instant noodles prepared in the dry goreng (stir-fried) style, topped with scallops, chicken cheese balls, crispy squid rings, and mixed vegetables. The dish is a crowd favourite that elevates the familiar Indomie instant noodle base with restaurant-quality seafood and meat toppings. The noodles are cooked and tossed in a savoury seasoning sauce, resulting in a chewy, flavourful base with a slightly smoky and umami-rich profile. The scallops add a sweet, tender seafood note, while the chicken cheese balls contribute a creamy, savoury richness. The squid rings provide a lightly chewy texture contrast, and the vegetables add freshness and colour. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate source of protein (~9 g per 100 g) from the seafood and chicken toppings, with a significant carbohydrate contribution from the noodle base (~18.5 g per 100 g). The sodium content is high (~520 mg per 100 g), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 53 (low category), but the large serving size results in a high glycemic load, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with shellfish, dairy, or gluten allergies. It is priced at SGD 17.90 and is available exclusively at The Noodle Place, Circular Road, Singapore.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional match found for Indomie Deluxe as served at The Noodle Place. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using HPB Singapore reference data for fried instant noodles and USDA FoodData Central data for seafood and chicken toppings. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cooked instant noodles. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and restaurant-specific preparation variability.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. Serving size of 450 g is estimated based on the description of a jumbo portion with multiple toppings; actual restaurant serving weight may vary. Halal status is listed as false because the chicken cheese ball and overall dish halal certification could not be confirmed from available sources; consumers should verify with the restaurant directly. The dish is served at The Noodle Place, Circular Road, Singapore, at SGD 17.90. Multilingual names for Chinese reflect the dish as premium dry fried noodles (豪华印度美炒面 / 豪華印度美炒麵). Indonesian local name retained as Indomie Deluxe given the brand origin. Tamil and Hindi transliterations are phonetic renderings of the English name.
